kbs.title: April 2019 Update for Windows 8.1 for x86-based and x64-based Systems (KB4496878)

cve_details.description: Monthly rollup containing multiple small security fixes; KB summary does not enumerate all CVEs. Enrich from MSRC / Security Update Guide for per-CVE details.

mitigation_hints.notes: Install monthly rollup MSU from Microsoft Update Catalog or via Windows Update.

kbs.notes: Monthly rollup. Per-CVE list not enumerated in the KB summary — enrich from MSRC / NVD for production use.

kbs.title: 2019-06 Security Monthly Quality Rollup for Windows 8.1 (KB4503276)

cve_details.description: Windows Hyper-V fails to properly validate certain inputs from a guest; may allow code execution on the host from a guest VM.
ActiveX Data Objects (ADO) memory handling issue allowing remote code execution via crafted input.
Jet Database Engine memory-handling vulnerability leading to possible remote code execution when parsing malicious database files.
Scripting engine memory corruption in Microsoft browsers leading to potential RCE via crafted web content.

mitigation_hints.notes: Install monthly rollup via MSU/Windows Update. For Hyper-V hosts consider isolating untrusted guests until patched.

kbs.notes: Monthly rollup — contains many fixes. Kernel-level CVEs were intentionally removed from `cve_details` to match your mitigation scope. See Microsoft KB for full per-CVE list. :contentReference[oaicite:3]{index=3}

kbs.title: 2019-06 Security-Only Quality Update for Windows 8.1 (KB4503290)

cve_details.description: Hyper-V host input validation issue allowing code execution from guest; security-only package contains the same fix as monthly rollup.
IIS request filtering mis-handling that may lead to Denial of Service in some configurations.

mitigation_hints.notes: Install security-only MSU from Microsoft Update Catalog; useful for minimal-security-only environments.

kbs.notes: Security-only package — contains the security fixes without the non-security quality changes. :contentReference[oaicite:4]{index=4}

kbs.title: 2019-07 Security Monthly Quality Rollup for Windows 8.1 (KB4507448)

cve_details.description: Hyper-V host input validation vulnerability fixed in July rollup — may allow host code execution from guest VM.
Remote code execution in a non-kernel Windows component due to improper memory handling; fixed in this rollup.

mitigation_hints.notes: Install monthly rollup MSU or use Windows Update. Ensure latest SSU before installing.

kbs.notes: Rollup supersedes previous monthly rollups. Kernel CVEs omitted per your constraint. :contentReference[oaicite:5]{index=5}

kbs.title: 2019-07 Security-Only Update for Windows 8.1 (KB4507457)

cve_details.description: Information disclosure / speculative execution related mitigation (Spectre/MDS family); OS-level mitigations applied.

mitigation_hints.notes: Install security-only MSU and apply vendor microcode updates if recommended.

kbs.notes: Security-only variant — contains security fixes only (no non-security quality updates).

kbs.title: 2019-08 Security Monthly Quality Rollup for Windows 8.1 / Server 2012 R2 (KB4512488)

cve_details.description: Non-kernel Windows remote code execution vulnerability fixed in August rollup.
Elevation-of-privilege in a Windows component (non-kernel) — local vector; fixed by update.

mitigation_hints.notes: Install monthly rollup MSU from Catalog or via Windows Update.

kbs.notes: Large August 2019 rollup; kernel CVEs omitted per your constraint. :contentReference[oaicite:6]{index=6}

kbs.title: 2019-08 Security-Only Update for Windows 8.1 (KB4512489)

cve_details.description: Non-kernel Windows remote code execution vulnerability fixed in the security-only package.

mitigation_hints.notes: Install security-only MSU from Microsoft Update Catalog.

kbs.notes: Security-only variant of August fixes. Kernel CVEs omitted.

kbs.title: 2019-09 Security-Only Quality Update for Windows 8.1 (KB4516064)

cve_details.description: Microarchitectural Store Buffer Data Sampling (MSBDS) — speculative execution side-channel; OS mitigations applied.

mitigation_hints.notes: Install security-only MSU; follow CPU vendor microcode guidance/practices where applicable.

kbs.notes: MDS / microarchitectural mitigations (OS-level). These are low-level mitigations that may also require CPU microcode updates; not kernel reverse-engineering but still low-level. :contentReference[oaicite:7]{index=7}

kbs.title: 2019-11 Security Monthly Quality Rollup for Windows 8.1 (KB4525243)

cve_details.description: Hyper-V Network Switch remote code execution vulnerability addressed in November rollup.
Local elevation-of-privilege in Print Spooler (splwow64.exe); patched in the rollup.
Jet Database Engine remote code execution via crafted database files; patched in this rollup.

mitigation_hints.notes: Install monthly rollup MSU from Microsoft Update Catalog.

kbs.notes: Kernel-level CVEs that appear in the full rollup (e.g., CVE-2019-11135) were intentionally excluded from `cve_details`. Enrich CVSS and exploit data from NVD/MSRC if you require accurate scoring.

kbs.title: 2019-11 Security-Only Update for Windows 8.1 (KB4525250)

cve_details.description: Hyper-V Network Switch RCE same as monthly rollup; found in the security-only package.

mitigation_hints.notes: Install security-only MSU from Microsoft Update Catalog.

kbs.notes: Security-only variant of the November monthly rollup; kernel CVEs excluded from `cve_details`.

kbs.title: March 9, 2021 — Monthly Rollup for Windows 8.1 (KB5000848)

cve_details.description: Monthly security rollup containing multiple fixes across Windows components; KB summary refers to Security Update Guide for per-CVE details.

kbs.notes: Per-CVE table is available in Microsoft's Security Update Guide; enrich for production use.

kbs.title: June 8, 2021 — Monthly Rollup for Windows 8.1 (KB5003671)

cve_details.description: June monthly rollup covering a broad set of Windows components (App Platform, Cloud Infrastructure, Authentication, Scripting Engine, etc.). See Security Update Guide for per-CVE mapping.

mitigation_hints.notes: Install monthly rollup MSU from Microsoft Update Catalog.

kbs.notes: Monthly rollup — expand per-CVE via Security Update Guide / NVD for scoring.

kbs.title: July 6, 2021 — Security-Only Update (Out-of-band) for Windows 8.1 (KB5004958)

cve_details.description: Windows Print Spooler improperly performs privileged file operations — remote code execution (PrintNightmare). An attacker could run arbitrary code as SYSTEM via crafted print requests or printer driver operations.

mitigation_hints.notes: Install security-only MSU; if patching not possible, disable Print Spooler service or restrict printer driver installation until patched.

kbs.notes: Out-of-band emergency update addressing PrintNightmare (actively exploited).

kbs.title: September 14, 2021 — Security-Only Update for Windows 8.1 (KB5005627)

cve_details.description: Windows Print Spooler remote code execution vulnerability (another PrintNightmare-related fix) — allows remote attackers to execute code via the spooler service.
MSHTML platform remote code execution: malicious Office documents or specially crafted web content can trigger remote code execution via MSHTML/IE rendering.
Windows remote code execution vulnerability allowing unauthorized commands; KB summary indicates remote exploitation potential.

mitigation_hints.notes: Install security-only MSU; apply Office/Browser hardening workarounds if patch cannot be immediately applied.

kbs.notes: Includes MSHTML/Print Spooler fixes and many application-level CVEs; kernel-level CVEs excluded.

kbs.title: September 14, 2021 — Monthly Rollup for Windows 8.1 (KB5005613)

cve_details.description: MSHTML remote code execution fixed in the September monthly rollup as well.

mitigation_hints.notes: Install monthly rollup MSU from Microsoft Update Catalog.

kbs.notes: Monthly rollup includes security-only fixes plus quality updates; supersedes the security-only where applicable.

kbs.title: December 14, 2021 — Security Update for Windows Media Center (KB5008285)

cve_details.description: Windows Media Center elevation of privilege vulnerability in Media Center components; local attacker could gain additional privileges.
Windows Media Center elevation of privilege vulnerability; fixed in this security update.

mitigation_hints.notes: Install KB MSU; consider disabling Media Center components on hosts that don’t need them.

kbs.notes: Many Media Center elevation-of-privilege CVEs patched; these are application-level and within mitigation scope.

kbs.title: December 11, 2018 — Security-Only Update for Windows 8.1 (KB4471322)

cve_details.description: .NET Framework denial of service: specially crafted web requests could cause denial of service in .NET applications.
.NET Framework remote code execution: input validation issue that could allow remote code execution in some hosted scenarios.
VBScript remote code execution vulnerability in Internet Explorer / scripting host.

mitigation_hints.notes: Install security-only MSU or apply targeted .NET/IE updates.

kbs.notes: This KB contains a mix of kernel and non-kernel CVEs. Kernel CVEs (e.g., CVE-2018-8477, CVE-2018-8611, CVE-2018-8641) were omitted from `cve_details` per your restriction; non-kernel CVEs retained above.

kbs.title: September 10, 2019 — Security-Only Quality Update for Windows 8.1 (KB4516064)

cve_details.description: Microarchitectural Store Buffer Data Sampling (MSBDS) — speculative-execution side-channel; OS-level mitigations applied.

mitigation_hints.notes: Install security-only MSU; also follow CPU vendor microcode guidance.

kbs.notes: These CVEs are microarchitectural MDS-class issues; included as 'os_update' mitigations. Kernel-mode CVEs (if any present in the KB) were excluded per your constraint.

kbs.title: September 10, 2019 — Monthly Rollup for Windows 8.1 (KB4516067)

cve_details.description: Microarchitectural Load Port Data Sampling (MLPDS) — speculative execution side-channel; OS mitigations included.

mitigation_hints.notes: Apply monthly rollup; ensure SSU prerequisites are installed.

kbs.notes: Monthly rollup includes MDS mitigations; kernel CVEs in the full rollup were removed from `cve_details` per your exclusion rules.

kbs.title: March 10, 2020 — Security Update (KB4541505)

cve_details.description: IIS request tampering — malformed headers could be used to tamper with requests (tampering / DoS risk depending on configuration).
Windows LNK processing vulnerability potentially allowing remote code execution when LNK files are processed.
cve_details.attack_vector: local / network (if LNK delivered via removable media or network share)

cve_details.description: Scripting engine memory handling leading to potential remote code execution via crafted web content.

mitigation_hints.notes: Install MSU; apply application hardening (disable scripting/IE/edge legacy features) if patching delayed.

kbs.notes: This KB contains a large mix of application and kernel CVEs. Kernel items (Win32k/CVE-2020-0788, CVE-2020-0799, CVE-2020-1114 etc.) were excluded from `cve_details`. Included above: prominent non-kernel CVEs (IIS, LNK, scripting engine).

kbs.title: May 12, 2020 — Security-Only Update for Windows 8.1 (KB4556853)

cve_details.description: Windows GDI information disclosure vulnerability affecting user-mode graphics components.
VBScript remote code execution vulnerability in the VBScript engine / Internet Explorer context.
Print Spooler elevation of privilege due to improper file operations; local attacker could gain elevated rights.
MSHTML engine remote code execution: crafted web content or documents may trigger RCE.

mitigation_hints.notes: Install security-only MSU; consider disabling components (IE/VBScript/Print Spooler) if immediate patching impossible.

kbs.notes: Some CVEs in this KB are kernel-level (CVE-2020-1054, CVE-2020-1114, etc.) and were excluded from `cve_details` per your kernel exclusion. The non-kernel CVEs above are included for mitigation.
